title: Experiencing Maori Culture in NZ with Kids — Best Cultural Experiences
meta_desc: Discover the best Maori cultural experiences in NZ for kids, from Te Puia to Waitangi
intro: Experiencing Maori culture is a highlight of any New Zealand trip, and with kids, it's essential to choose attractions that are both fun and educational. Te Puia in Rotorua is a great place to start, with its mix of geothermal wonders and traditional Maori performances. For a truly authentic experience, consider visiting a local marae or attending a cultural festival

sections.heading: Top Maori Cultural Attractions
list: Te Puia: $50-$70 NZD per person for a guided tour
Mitai Maori Village: $115-$145 NZD per person for a dinner and show package
Waitangi Treaty Grounds: $50-$70 NZD per person for a guided tour
Whakarewarewa Maori Village: $35-$50 NZD per person for a self-guided tour

sections.heading: Authentic vs Tourist Experiences
sections.body: When choosing a Maori cultural experience, it's essential to consider what's authentic and what's tailored for tourists. For example, Hobbiton Movie Set is a popular attraction, but it's not a traditional Maori experience. On the other hand, visiting a local marae or attending a cultural festival can provide a more genuine insight into Maori culture.

sections.heading: Getting There
sections.body: Drive times from major cities: Auckland to Rotorua (3 hours), Wellington to Rotorua (5.5 hours), Queenstown to Rotorua (9 hours). Consider renting a campervan or booking a guided tour to make the most of your trip.

cta.text: Use our NZ family travel budget calculator

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
list: Campervans: rent from $100-$200 NZD per day
Holiday parks: $30-$70 NZD per night for a family cabin
Hotels: $150-$300 NZD per night for a family room
